changes_summary: Revised the plan around the two critique flags: epic selection is now explicitly pre-turn and lock-safe in `resident.py`/`loop.py`, and search/list tools now go through the Store protocol with both SQLite and Supabase migrations/adapters instead of a Supabase-only path.
questions: Which timestamp should define “most recently edited epic”: explicit epic `updated_at`, last message timestamp, last bot-applied mutation timestamp, or another project-specific field?
Should full-text search include all messages visible to the current user/conversation, or only messages attached to active epics?
For reference resolution, is it acceptable to store structured metadata for future bot outputs, or must Sprint 3 resolve references only from already-stored raw text?
What exact conversation gap threshold should trigger acknowledgment if `planning-bot-spec.md` leaves it flexible?
Should user mode be sticky across turns until changed, or apply only to the message where the mode is detected?

success_criteria.criterion: Ambiguous messages with five active epics select the most recently edited active epic within 24 hours when exactly one qualifying default exists.
Epic selection occurs before `run_turn` lock acquisition, or an explicitly tested safe switch path re-acquires the selected epic lock before any mutation.
Inbound and outbound message rows for a routed real epic are attached to the selected epic rather than left on the synthetic/default Discord conversation key.
When no clear epic context exists, the bot asks a clarification question instead of mutating or responding under a guessed real epic.
Switching from one epic to another includes the destination epic title in the outbound message.
`list_epics`, `search_epics`, and `search_messages` are implemented as Store protocol methods and exposed as tools without bypassing `agent_kit/ports.py`.
SQLite and Supabase store adapters both support `list_epics`, `search_epics`, and `search_messages` with compatible result shapes.
Both SQLite and Supabase migrations add indexed full-text search or the repository's established equivalent for `messages.content`.
`search_messages` returns the expected hit IDs for 10 seeded full-text search queries in the supported store test mode or contract suite.
Reference phrases such as “the second one” and “that point” resolve against the last outbound bot message in at least 9 of 10 unit fixtures.
“show me what you know about X” returns a structured summary containing every section required by `planning-bot-spec.md`.
Mode detection produces distinct response policy inputs for Deep-thinking, Brainstorming, and Executing.
LLM-graded epic-selection eval reaches at least 27 correct out of 30 canned scenarios.
LLM-graded ambiguity eval verifies the bot asks rather than guesses on 10 deliberately ambiguous cases.
Existing repository tests continue to pass after Sprint 3 changes.
Search and selection logic is kept in deterministic modules with focused unit tests rather than embedded only in prompts.
Tool outputs contain enough context for disambiguation without exposing unnecessary internal or cross-user data.
Manual product review confirms the three user modes feel meaningfully different without becoming verbose or performative.

assumptions: The critique's file paths reflect the current repo shape: `agent_kit/transport/discord.py`, `agent_kit/resident.py`, `agent_kit/loop.py`, `agent_kit/ports.py`, `agent_kit/store/sqlite.py`, and `agent_kit/store/supabase.py`.
The service-role key from the brief must not be committed, logged, or embedded in tests; local tests should use existing environment configuration or test credentials.
The 24h default applies only when the user message is ambiguous; explicit epic names or corrections override recency.
Active epics exclude archived/done/deleted epics if such status fields exist.
Reference resolution should prefer the current conversation's last outbound bot message, scoped to the selected epic/conversation context.
Search tools must work in both invocation/local SQLite mode and Supabase mode via the Store abstraction.
If inbound Discord messages are persisted before final epic routing, the implementation may add a minimal Store method to reassign the inbound message to the selected epic before `run_turn`.
